title: 11 Palestinian Families Forcibly Evicted from Silwan's Batn al-Hawa — East Jerusalem
detail: At least 11 Palestinian families were forcibly evicted from the Batn al-Hawa neighborhood of Silwan, occupied East Jerusalem. Their homes were transferred to the Israeli settler organization Ateret Cohanim under a 1970 Israeli law granting Jews exclusive property-reclaim rights for pre-1948 property — with no reciprocal right for Palestinians. B'Tselem stated: 'Amidst the ongoing illegal and lethal Israeli-American offensive against Iran, Israel is expanding its ethnic cleansing in East Jerusalem, throwing Palestinian families into the streets.' Norwegian Refugee Council and Ir Amim confirmed the evictions. At-risk population: approximately 90 families (700 people) in Batn al-Hawa face imminent displacement; 1,500 people from 150 families in Silwan's al-Bustan area are also threatened.

sources.name: Al Jazeera — Israel forcibly displaces more Palestinian families in East Jerusalem, 25 March 2026
B'Tselem — East Jerusalem ethnic cleansing documentation, March 2026
Norwegian Refugee Council — East Jerusalem evictions confirmation, March 2026
Ir Amim — Silwan displacement report, March 2026

title: IDF Soldiers Kill Palestinian Worker, Wound Three During Vehicle Pursuit Near Checkpoint in Hebron Hills
detail: IDF soldiers shot at vehicles transporting Palestinian workers during a pursuit near a military checkpoint in the southern Hebron Hills. One Palestinian was killed and three others were wounded, including one by live fire. Footage showed the vehicles traveling away from a checkpoint at the time of the shooting. An eyewitness said the man killed had reentered the vehicle before it overturned. The Palestinian Red Crescent Society confirmed the casualty figures. Haaretz reported the incident as raising questions about IDF rules of engagement in the West Bank.

sources.name: Haaretz — Palestinian killed after IDF soldiers fire at vehicle during West Bank pursuit, 25 March 2026
Palestinian Red Crescent Society — Casualty confirmation, March 25, 2026
WAFA — Palestinian Authority news agency, March 25, 2026

title: Foreign Policy: Israel Advancing End to Palestinian Self-Determination Amid Iran War
detail: Foreign Policy published an analysis warning that under cover of the US-Israel war on Iran, Israel is pursuing a coordinated strategy to permanently end Palestinian self-determination. The report noted that talks for implementing Trump's 20-point Gaza peace plan have 'slowed dramatically,' with no resolution on connections between Gaza and the West Bank, the mandate of the National Committee for the Administration of Gaza (NCAG), or Hamas's future status. The Palestinian Authority faces a deepening financial crisis as Israel continues withholding nearly $2.5 billion of Palestinian clearance revenue. Israeli authorities approved 19 new settlements in December 2025 and issued tenders for thousands of units in the E1 area northeast of Jerusalem, a corridor whose development would sever East Jerusalem from the West Bank. The UN Security Council Deputy Special Coordinator called for consolidation of the Gaza ceasefire and a halt to settlement expansion.

sources.name: Foreign Policy — Amid a Wider War, Israel Is Continuing Its Push to End Palestinian Self-Determination, 25 March 2026
UN Security Council — Consolidate Gaza Ceasefire, Halt Escalating Violence press release (SC16284)
Al Jazeera — What's happened in Gaza and the West Bank since the start of the Iran war, 13 March 2026
